agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Nonprofits receiving larger contributions are more likely to be located in wealthier ZIP codes with higher home values (ZHVI).
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Wolch (1990) and Bielefeld (2000) show that nonprofit funding resources and headquarters locations are highly correlated with localized regional wealth and property values.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant but practically very weak positive correlation (beta = 0.00005) was found. While larger nonprofits tend to be located in higher-cost ZIP codes, the effect size is extremely tiny, indicating local real estate prices are primarily driven by broader housing market dynamics rather than nonprofit contributions.
